columns.name: employee_id
columns.description: Unique employee identifier; primary key and component of the merge key (employee_id + updated_date) used by sp_mysqltobq_load.sql for upsert deduplication.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Employee email address; PII field — consider BigQuery column-level security policy tags.

columns.name: phone_number
columns.description: Employee phone number; optional; PII field — consider BigQuery column-level security policy tags.

columns.name: job_id
columns.description: Job role code; used as a clustering column to optimise job-based analytical queries.

columns.name: salary
columns.description: Employee base salary with two decimal places; sensitive compensation data.

columns.name: commission_pct
columns.description: Commission percentage applicable to the employee; NULL indicates a non-commissioned role.

columns.name: manager_id
columns.description: employee_id of the direct manager; self-referential foreign key. NULL for top-level managers.

columns.name: department_id
columns.description: Department identifier; first clustering column to prune partitions for department-scoped queries.

columns.name: status
columns.description: Employment status (e.g. ACTIVE, INACTIVE); second clustering column for status-filtered queries.

columns.name: created_date
columns.description: Timestamp when the record was first created in the MySQL source system (UTC).

columns.name: updated_date
columns.description: Timestamp of the last update in MySQL; partition column (DAY granularity) and second component of the sp_mysqltobq_load.sql merge key. NULL rows land in the BigQuery __NULL__ partition.

cardinality.notes: Many employees may report to one manager via the manager_id → employee_id self-join. Top-level managers have a NULL manager_id.
